issues.description: CartContext.tsx exports two components: CartProvider (line 40) and useCart hook (line 125). While useCart is technically a hook not a component, having a Context Provider component and a hook in the same file creates a multi-export pattern. This is a common and accepted pattern for Context files, but it's worth noting.
issues.fix: Context+hook pairing in the same file is an accepted pattern (similar to compound components exception). No action needed unless the file grows significantly.

issues.description: FlyToCartContext.tsx exports two functions: FlyToCartProvider (line 11) and useFlyToCartContext (line 21). Same pattern as CartContext.tsx - context+hook pairing.
issues.fix: Context+hook co-location is acceptable convention. No action needed.

patterns.description: InlineTagEditor.tsx correctly uses isInputVisible (renamed from showInput in cycle 3). The test InlineTagEditor.test.tsx also correctly tests the isInputVisible behavior by clicking the add button and checking input visibility.
Standard component structure (hooks → derived → handlers → render) is consistently followed across reviewed components. usePaymentMethodsManager is a well-structured hook.
Container/Presenter pattern is properly used in key components: ProductListPage (data) + ProductListPageContent (presentation), CheckoutPage + CheckoutPageContent, etc.
